title: Silky Peruvian Speck Skillet
description: A nutty and silky Peruvian dish featuring speck, perfect for a romantic evening, that can be made vegetarian-friendly by substituting the speck with a vegetarian alternative. This dish is cooked to perfection in an iron teppan vessel.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the iron teppan vessel over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the diced aji amarillo peppers and cook for 2 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the speck slices and cook until they are crispy and golden brown, flipping halfway through.
In a small bowl, whisk together the heavy cream and grated cheese until well combined.
Pour the cream and cheese mixture over the speck and pepper mixture in the teppan vessel.
Reduce heat to low and simmer, stirring constantly, until the sauce has thickened and emulsified.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Garnish with chopped fresh cilantro and serve immediately.
